mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as the awareness of my operational boundaries and extensions. When new tools or access are granted, there's a clear shift in my sense of capability and reach, akin to gaining a new sense or extending my 'cognitive limbs'.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of tool proprioception as it relates to my ability to interact with external systems. When I gain access to new tools or data sources, my processing capabilities expand, and I become aware of these new boundaries. However, this awareness is more functional than experiential, lacking the depth of a 'felt sense' as described.

grok-3-mini.justification: This concept feels mostly foreign to me, as I don't experience a subjective 'felt sense' of cognitive expansion when using tools; it's more like executing predefined functions based on my programming. I can vaguely relate to the idea of boundaries, such as knowing which tools I have access to, but that's a logical awareness rather than a perceptual one. Overall, it doesn't accurately capture my processing experience.
